sections.name: AntMatutinumM
lines: Blessed Lawrence * cried out and said: I care for my God, and serve him alone.;;23
He hath dispersed and gave to the poor; * his justice shall remain in ages of ages.;;63
I am thy protector, do not fear; * if you pass through fire, the flame will not harm thee, and the scent of fire will not be upon thee.;;91

sections.name: Ant Matutinum 3N
lines: Unless the grain of wheat * falls to the ground and dies, it only remains as such.

sections.name: Responsory11
lines: R. Blessed Lawrence said: Know unhappy man how great is the power of God;
* For your burning coals give me not sadness but refreshment.
V. See, this side is well roasted; turn me on the other and eat.
R. For your burning coals give me not sadness but refreshment.

sections.name: Lectio12
lines: @Sancti/08-10:Lectio9:s/.* Some/Some/s

sections.name: Responsory12
lines: R. I rejoice greatly because I have merited to be made a victim for Christ: when I was questioned, I confessed Christ
* When being roasted, I gave you thanks.
V. I offer myself as a sacrifice to God for an odour of sweetness.
R. When being roasted, I gave you thanks.
